explanation: The child is sleeping on their back in a crib with their body positioned at a slight tilt, with a thicker-looking blanket positioned just below the arms and across the middle of the body. Soft objects, including stuffed animals, are also located above the child’s head about one foot away. Because the blanket is positioned higher on the body rather than around the lower area and appears thicker, if it shifts toward the face during sleep, it would present a more serious safety concern.
